title: Cohesion and reference
explanation:
Cohesion and reference

C1 texts use reference chains, substitution and conjunction precisely.

  • this phenomenon, the former, the latter, such measures
  • do so, one, ones, the same
  • thereby, hence, accordingly

questions.text: Two policies were proposed; [*the former|the ladder|the weather] focused on tax, [*the latter|the former|the ceiling] on spending.
The board approved the plan and [*did so|did it|did this] without delay.
questions.feedback: Did so substitutes approved the plan.

questions.text: If regulations fail, [*such measures|such weather|such music] may lose public support.
The first model is cheaper; I prefer [*that one|that ones|those one].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The experiment failed twice. ____ , the team redesigned the method. | *Accordingly | Nevertheless | For example
Three options were discussed, but only two of ____ were realistic. | *them | it | this
She supported the proposal, and he did ____. | *likewise | never | backward
The policy reduced emissions and ____ improved air quality. | *thereby | although | whereas
The report criticised delays; [*this criticism|this weather|this colour] angered officials.
I need a copy of the contract - do you have [*one|ones|it]?
Sales improved in Q1; [*by contrast|for instance|as usual] Q2 was weaker.
They reached the same conclusion and wrote [*the same|same the|same it] in the summary.
The law was vague; [*as a result|for example|on the other hand] courts interpreted it differently.
